title: StylizedDamage
description: A damage number display mod, offering a highly customizable and buttery-smooth experience.
body:
# StylizedDamage - A Different Kind of Damage Number

## Description

A damage number display mod that offers a highly customizable and buttery-smooth experience.

You can bind different visual styles to different damage types, with support for custom prefixes, suffixes, and small icons.

Features kill pop-ups, a total damage display in the top-right corner, and intelligent damage number positioning.

## Technical Highlights

Implemented using a **World-to-HUD** approach, allowing you to see damage numbers through blocks and from any direction.

- Numbers emerge exactly where they should — from your crosshair during melee attacks, or from the projectile's position for ranged attacks. Unlike other damage number mods that simply float above entities' heads.
- Compatible with various camera angles and FOV settings.
- Works with zoom effects such as telescopes and scope attachments from gun mods.
- Smooth movement and scaling.
- Performance optimized.

## Highly Customizable

This mod uses a **Selector + Style** system.

In the **Selector**, you can assign specific number styles based on damage magnitude, team affiliation, entity type, and damage type.

In the **Style**, you can customize the number's appearance, content, and animation:

- Adjust number size and linger duration based on damage value.
- Add custom prefixes, suffixes, and icons.
- Build your own animations from four components: position, scale, opacity, and brightness.

## Total Damage Display

A total damage number is shown in the top-right corner, with a trailing list of recent damage instances.

The trailing damage numbers support custom styles and feature dynamic animations that enhance the feeling of impact.

## Integration
This mod comes with pre-configured styles and selector settings for Iron's Spells 'n Spellbooks, ready to use out of the box. See Gallery Image 1 for the effect.

## Start Using It Now

Everything you need is right here:  
https://yanbwe.github.io/Yanbwe-Wiki/en/stylizeddamage/
